docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
1. How to view the log of the system updates?

  2. On the menu, click the ‘Help’ and then the ‘Changelog’ sub-menu

  3. The ‘Changelog’ page is displayed in a new tab.
  4. Changelog provides details about every updates rolled out.

docs.title: FAQs
docs.text:
1. What is the function of the Time Tracker (TT) app?

    The TT app monitors and tracks the amount of time spent on various tasks or activities while also capturing screenshots of your screen.

  2. What if I am using dual screens?

    In a dual screen setup, screenshots are exclusively captured on the main display of the PC.

  3. What if I am using multiple desktop feature in Windows 11?

    While utilizing the multiple desktop feature in Windows 11, it is important to note that any of your active desktops displayed on your screen can be captured by the TT app.

  4. What are the minimum system and network requirements for using the TT app?

    • The TT app should be used with either a wired broadband connection or a WiFi connection.
    • The user’s computer should have at least 20% available RAM to ensure smooth operation of the TT app.
    • It is important to start the TT app only when connected to the internet.

  5. How do I report ‘missing hours’ from my Timesheet?

    • If you have any ‘missing hours’ in your Timesheet, please submit a ticket through the provided link to notify our IT team about the issue.
    • To initiate a thorough investigation of the issue and prevent any future problems, kindly allow our IT team to examine your system.
    • Following the inspection, our IT team will assess the files on our server to determine the feasibility of rolling back the missing hours.
    • Please note that providing evidence of work, such as Bundy Clock records or Screenshot of work is essential for the validation of your request to roll back the missing hours. Failure to furnish proof of work will render your rollback request invalid.

  6. Which version of the Time Tracker App should I use?

    • The TT app is accessible across multiple platforms, including Windows, Mac, and Linux.
    • For Windows, the app is available in 64-bit format.
    • For MAC, the app is available as a .dmg file.
    • For Linux, the app is available as a JAR file.
    • The latest version of the TT app is X.X.XX (Refer website)

  7. How can I determine the appropriate TT format for my Windows operating system?

    • For Windows 7 users, navigate to ‘My Computer’ and right-click on ‘Properties’ to access your System type details (32 or 64-bit OS).
    • For Windows 8 or newer versions, go to ‘My PC’ and right-click on ‘Properties’ to ascertain your System type details (32 or 64-bit OS).
    • NOTE: If you don’t have the latest version installed, you can download it from the following link

  8. How do I install the Time Tracker app on my ‘Mac’?

    • Download the latest version of the TT app for Mac from this link
    • Uninstall any existing or old TT app on your Mac by following the instructions provided in this video
    • Open the downloaded TT app installer package and grant permission as guided in this video

    By following these instructions, you should be able to successfully install the TT app on your Mac.

  9. What steps should be taken if the ‘Projects and Tasks’ fail to load in the TT app?

    • Verify your internet connection to ensure it is stable and functioning properly.
    • Click the ‘Refresh Projects & Tasks’ button within the TT app to reload the lists and check if the issue is resolved.
    • If the problem persists, please proceed to open a ticket by visiting the following link

  10. What should be done if the TT app becomes unresponsive or freezes?

    • Open the 'Task Manager' on your computer.
    • Locate and select the TT app from the list of the running applications, then choose the ‘End Task’ option.Additionally, please ensure to check the ‘Background processes’ section in the Task Manager.
    • Verify your internet connection to ensure it is stable and functioning properly.
    • Double-check your 'Timesheet' for any missing hours or discrepancies.
    • Reach out to one of the IT staff members via Skype messaging for further assistance.
    • Please open a ticket by visiting the following link

  11. How can you determine the version of your TT app?

    • Open the TT app on your device.
    • Look for the top-left corner of the app window. The version information should be displayed in that area.
    • For visual reference, you can visit the following link

    By following these instructions and referring to the provided image, you should be able to locate and identify the version of your TT app. The latest version is X.X.X.X

  12. What can be done if the loading of Projects and Tasks in the TT app is slow?

    The loading speed of Projects & Tasks in the TT app is influenced by the following factors: Internet Connection: Ensure that you have a stable and reliable internet connection.

    • Navigate to the control panel.
    • Access the ‘Network & internet’ section then ‘View network status and tasks’.
    • Check your basic network information and configure connections as needed.

  13. What are the steps to add a ‘New Task’ in the TT app?

    • Open the TT app.
    • Locate and click on the ‘New Task’ button.
    • Enter the necessary details for the new task and proceed to click the Save button.

  14. What information regarding dates and times is displayed on the TT app?

    • In the TT app, the dates and times are presented in Eastern Time (ET), as follows:
    • The displayed information indicates the date and time at which the user launched the TT app.
    • By default, the displayed date and time correspond to when the user initiates the TT app timer. The color indicator for this state is gray. When the user stops the timer, the color indicator changes to orange. In cases where the TT app stops due to internet problems, idle time-outs, or time jumps, the color indicator updates to red.
    • The provided data represents the current date and time clock.

  15. What steps can be taken to verify both offline and online Timesheets?

    If you have concerns about the completeness of your record, you can cross-reference your offline and online Timesheets. Follow these steps to check your offline Timesheet:

    • Open the TT desktop app and locate the ‘Offline Timesheet’ button, positioned below the ‘Log Out’ button.
    • Click on the ‘Offline Timesheet’ button to open the ‘Locally Stored Timesheet’ dialog box.
    • Select the desired week-ending date that you wish to review from the available options.
    • Click the ‘Show’ button to display the offline Timesheet for the selected week-ending date.
    • Cross-check the offline Timesheet with the corresponding online Timesheet.

    To check your online Timesheet, follow these steps:

    • Go to https://timetracker.20four7va.co/ and log in using your credentials.
    • Navigate to the Reports menu and select Timesheets.
    • Adjust the Date field to correspond to the desired week ending you wish to check, then click the refresh icon to update the information
    • Scroll down to the ‘Weekly Timesheet’ section and review the recorded data.
    • Cross-reference this information with your offline Timesheet to ensure accuracy.

    By following these instructions, you can access and compare the online Timesheet from the Reports menu with your offline records, allowing you to double-check the accuracy and completeness of your time entries.

  16. What to do when ‘offline and online Timesheets’ do not match?

    After cross-checking the Timesheets and identifying logs that are not synced online, users can utilize the ‘Sync from here’ button within the ‘Locally Stored Timesheet’ dialog box to initiate an automatic sync/upload process for those specific logs.

docs.title: Welcome to MkDocs
docs.text: For full documentation visit mkdocs.org.

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Install Java JDK 21 (Windows, macOS, Linux)
docs.text:
Purpose

Install Java Development Kit (JDK 21) for development and runtime execution across environments.

Reference:

Official Oracle Downloads: Download Java JDK

⸻

Prerequisites

•   Admin / sudo access
•   Internet connection
•   Compatible OS (Windows, macOS, Linux)

⸻

Supported Environments

•   Windows (x64)
•   macOS (Intel / Apple Silicon)
•   Linux (Debian, Ubuntu, RHEL, etc.)

Steps

Step 1: Download JDK 21

•   Go to Oracle download page
•   Accept license agreement
•   Download based on OS:

OS File Type

Windows  .msi
macOS    .dmg
Linux    .tar.gz / .deb / .rpm

docs.title: Windows Installation
docs.text:
Step 2: Install JDK

Double-click jdk-21_windows-x64_bin.msi

•   Follow installer wizard
•   Click Next → Install → Finish

Step 3: (Optional) Silent Install

msiexec.exe /i jdk-21_windows-x64_bin.msi

Step 4: Set Environment Variables

•   Go to: System → Advanced → Environment Variables

Add: 

JAVA_HOME = C:\Program Files\Java\jdk-21

Update PATH: 

%JAVA_HOME%\bin

docs.title: macOS Installation
docs.text:
Step 2: Install JDK - Double-click downloaded .dmg file

Then:

Double-click JDK 21.pkg → Continue → Install

Installer wizard completes installation

Step 3: Configure PATH Open Terminal and paste the following:

echo 'export PATH="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/jdk-21.jdk/Contents/Home/bin:$PATH"' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc

docs.title: Linux Installation Option A:
docs.text:
Using tar.gz

cd /opt
sudo tar -xvzf jdk-21_linux-x64_bin.tar.gz

JDK will be extracted into a directory like: /opt/jdk-21 ``` oai_citation:2‡Oracle Docs

docs.title: Option B: Debian/Ubuntu (.deb)
docs.text:
```bash
    sudo dpkg -i jdk-21_linux-x64_bin.deb

Step 3: Set Environment Variables Open Terminal and run the following:

export JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/jdk-21
export PATH=$JAVA_HOME/bin:$PATH

Verification Run in terminal:

java -version

Expected Output: java version "21"

docs.title: Common issues
docs.text:
Issue: java command not found

Fix:

export PATH=$PATH:$JAVA_HOME/bin

Issue: Multiple Java versions conflict

Fix:

update-alternatives --config java

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text:
Windows Uninstall via Control Panel

macOS

sudo rm -rf /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/jdk-21.jdk

Linux

sudo rm -rf /usr/lib/jvm/jdk-21

docs.title: Manual logs
docs.text:
This page deals with the different logs of the user such as the manual logs, overtime logs, and leaves. You could file the said logs on this page and monitor for its result if it’s approved or not.

  1. How to add a manual log?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Manual Logs’ and then the ‘Add Manual Log’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Manual Log’ page is displayed.
    • Fill out all the required fields on the ‘Manual Log’ page and click the ‘Save And Upload’ button.
    • Upon providing all the required fields and clicking the ‘Save And Upload’ button on the ‘Manual Log’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.

  2. How to view added manual logs?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Manual Log’ and then the ‘Manage Manual Logs’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Manage Manual Logs’ page is displayed. You may edit the ‘From’ and ‘To’ date pickers to filter the manual logs search result and check for its ‘Status’.

  3. How to add overtime hours?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Manual Logs’ and then the ‘Add Overtime Hours’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Over Time Log’ page is displayed.
    • Fill out all the required fields on the ‘Over Time Log’ page and click the ‘Save And Upload’ button.
    • Upon providing all the required fields and clicking the ‘Save And Upload’ button on the ‘Over Time Log’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.

  4. How to view added overtime logs?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Manual Log’ and then the ‘Manage Overtime Logs’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Manage Overtime Logs’ page is displayed. You may edit the ‘From’ and ‘To’ date pickers to filter the overtime logs search result and check for its ‘Status’.

  5. How to apply for a leave?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Manual Logs’ and then the ‘Apply for Leave’ sub-menu.
    • The 'Apply for Leave’ page is displayed.
    • Fill out all the required fields on the ‘Apply for Leave’ page and click the ‘Submit Leave Application’ button.
    • Upon providing all the required fields and clicking the ‘Submit Leave Application’ on the ‘Apply for Leave’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.

  6. How to view applied leaves?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Manual Log’ and then the ‘Manage Leaves’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Manage Leaves’ page is displayed. You may edit the ‘From’ and ‘To’ date pickers to filter the leaves logs search result and check for its ‘Status’.

    The ‘Reports’ page is where the users could access their End of Day (EOD) Report, Submit Speed Test, TimeSheets, and other reports.

  7. How to send your EOD report?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Reports’ and then the ‘Send My EOD’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Send your EOD’ page is displayed.
    • Pick a date and click on the ‘Refresh’ button.
    • Fill out all the other fields (required or optional) on the ‘Send your EOD’ page and click the Submit button.
    • Upon submitting the form on the ‘Send your EOD’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed. To submit another EOD Report, you may pick another date again, hit the ‘Refresh’ button, and fill out the form.

  8. How to submit your speed test?

    • On the menu, click the ‘Reports’ and then the ‘Submit Speed Test’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Speedtest’ page is displayed.
    • Run the speed test by clicking the ‘GO’ button, copy the link and paste it on the ‘Speed Test Result Link:’ input field.
    • Fill out the ‘Internet Service Provider Details’ form and click the ‘Submit Details’ button. Make sure to enter both the ‘Main’ and ‘Backup Internet Connection’ types.
    • Upon submitting the form on the ‘Speedtest’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.

    NOTE: Every first day of the month, the system will ask for the main and backup internet speed test details of the user before fully utilizing the system menus.

docs.title: Reports
docs.text:
The ‘Reports’ page is where the users could access their End of Day (EOD) Report, Submit Speed Test, TimeSheets, and other reports.

  1. How to send your EOD report?

  2. On the menu, click the ‘Reports’ and then the ‘Send My EOD’ sub-menu.

  3. The ‘Send your EOD’ page is displayed.
  4. Pick a date and click on the ‘Refresh’ button
  5. Fill out all the other fields (required or optional) on the ‘Send your EOD’ page and click the Submit button.
  6. Upon submitting the form on the ‘Send your EOD’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed. To submit another EOD Report, you may pick another date again, hit the ‘Refresh’ button, and fill out the form.

docs.title: Screenshots
docs.text:
This page allows the user to view their tasks, work hours, and screenshots for a specified time. It could be filtered daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly.

NOTE: Server automatically deletes screenshots that are more than three (3) weeks old.

docs.title: How to view screenshots?
docs.text:
1. On the ‘View Screenshots’ page, enter the desired ‘From Date’ and ‘To Date’ and click the ‘Refresh’ button.

  2. Once the page reloads, choose the entry that you wish to view the captured screenshots on the table.

  3. Click on the corresponding ‘Actions (...)’ button and select the ‘View Screenshots’ option.

  4. The ‘Screenshots’ page is displayed. You may click on the ‘Back to Session List’ button to view other recorded screenshots.

docs.title: How to delete screenshots?
docs.text:
1. On the ‘View Screenshots’ page, enter the desired ‘From Date’ and ‘To Date’ and click the ‘Refresh’ button.

  2. Once the page reloads, choose the entry that you wish to delete a/the captured screenshot/s on the table.

  3. Click on the corresponding ‘Actions (...)’ button and select the ‘View Screenshots’ option.

  4. On the ‘Screenshots’ page, click the corresponding ‘Delete’ icon of the screenshot/s that you wish to delete.

A pop-up message is displayed. Click the ‘OK’ button to continue deleting or the ‘Cancel’ button to cancel the process. Upon clicking the ‘OK’ button, a successful deletion confirmation message should be displayed on the screen.

docs.title: How to delete a session?
docs.text:
1. On the ‘View Screenshots’ page, enter the desired ‘From Date’ and ‘To Date’ and click the ‘Refresh’ button.

  2. Once the page reloads, choose the session that you wish to delete on the table.

  3. Click on the corresponding ‘Actions (...)’ button and select the ‘Delete Session’ option.

A pop-up message is displayed. Click the ‘OK’ button to continue deleting or the ‘Cancel’ button to cancel the process (Figure 14). Upon clicking the ‘OK’ button, a successful deletion confirmation message should be displayed on the screen.

docs.title: Submit Ticket
docs.text:
1. How to submit a ticket to the IT Department ?

  2. On the menu, click the ‘Help’ and then the ‘Submit Ticket’ sub-menu.

  3. The ‘Create Ticket’ page is displayed.
  4. Fill out all the required fields on the ‘Create Ticket’ page and click the ‘Create Ticket’ button.
  5. Upon providing all the required fields and clicking the ‘Create Ticket’ button on the ‘Create Ticket’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.

NOTE: If the form is not working, you can drop an email to codestacks@20four7va.com

docs.title: How to read the site Dashboard?
docs.text:
• Autolog Summary

    This is the chart representation of the Total Hours of Work of the day

  • Manual Log Summary

    This is the chart representation of the Manual Hours Work of the week.

  • Leave Summary

    This is the chart representation of the Available Paid Time-Offs for one whole year.

  • Total Hours Today

    This shows the total work hours recorded on the 20four7VA Time Tracker Desktop App of the day.

  • Manual Hours This Week

    This shows the total approved manual hours of the week.

  • Available PTOs

    This shows the total available paid time-offs for one whole year.

  • Weekly Timesheet

    This shows the total weekly timesheet record of the user.

  • My Clients

    This shows the client(s) details under the user.

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text:
The 20four7VA TimeTracker is a proprietary software made in order to manage the user's work hours in a more robust and smooth manner. It will help managers and clients track and monitor the VA's work on a regular basis.

Software OS Versions - The TimeTracker comes in 3 operating system versions as of now, Windows (7,8,10), Mac and Linux.

Download - In order to Download the Windows / Mac / Linux version, users can type in: https://timetracker.20four7va.co in the address bar and go to the Menu Downloads > Windows/ Mac/Linux as per their computer systems.

docs.title: INSTALLATION PROCEDURE
docs.text:
1. Once you click on the Downloads > Windows section from the top menu bar on site it will take you to the following page:

  2. On the ‘Download’ Windows File page. Click on the ‘Download’ button and the software will download in the bottom left corner of the screen as shown below:

Note: We are taking Chrome Browser as a Standard. The download location can be different if you use any other browsers like Firefox, Safari, Opera, or IE.

  1. Once the download has been completed, click it to open the downloaded executable file. This pop-up message might be displayed. Click on the ‘More info’ link and click on the ‘Run anyway’ button.

  2. Click on the ‘Install’ and the software will install automatically. The login screen will automatically show up, as well. Enter the username & password that was sent to your webmail account and click on ‘Login’.

  3. The app will load relevant projects and tasks and you will see the following screen post-loading.

  4. Before you start the timer you must Select a ‘Project’ and its associated ‘Task’. Then finally click on ‘Start Timer’ .

  5. You are done. Congrats! TimeTracker will start recording & tracking your system!

  6. Upon completion of your daily work / while taking a break you can click on ‘Stop Timer’ & click on ‘Log Out’.

docs.title: User profile
docs.text:
On this page, the user could update their profile information, password, check leaves, and could be able to log out of the system.

  1. How to update the user profile information ?

    • On the menu, click the ‘user name’ and then the ‘Update Profile’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Update Profile’ page is displayed.
    • Review and update the profile information that you wish to change on the ‘Update Profile’ page and click the ‘Save Changes’ button.
    • Upon updating the profile information that you wish to change and clicking the ‘Save Changes’ button on the ‘Update Profile’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.

  2. How to update the user password ?

    • On the menu, click the ‘user name’ and then the ‘Change Password’ sub-menu.
    • The ‘Change Password’ page is displayed.
    • Fill out the ‘New Password’ and ‘Confirm New Password’ text fields and click the ‘Update Password’ button.
    • Upon entering the ‘New Password’ and clicking the ‘Update Password’ button on the ‘Change Password’ page, a successful confirmation message is displayed.
